action.reasoning: Observation: My predict.py runs successfully producing 18000 predictions, but the evaluator.py fails looking for '/data/test_labels.txt'. This has happened consistently across multiple eval submissions. My model has dev EER=0.0012. Analysis: The eval framework issue seems persistent. However, I notice the eval error shows my predict.py correctly finds data at /rab_data/asvspoof2021_la_test/ and produces predictions. The evaluator then fails on its own labels file. This might be a framework configuration issue that gets resolved. Meanwhile, I should try WavLM-large which is known to generalize better for anti-spoofing tasks. Decision: Submit eval again (might work this time), and also try training with WavLM-large backbone which has better representations for codec-degraded audio.
